---
title: "nitrix.stats.gaulss"
generated: true
signature-hash: 7d872a2e15dece35
description: "Gaussian location-scale regression (`gaulss`)."
listing:
  id: nitrix-stats-gaulss-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-stats-gaulss/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.gaulss`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Gaussian location-scale regression (`gaulss`).

The standard generalised linear model has a single linear predictor (the mean);
the residual scale is a single nuisance dispersion.  A *distributional* model
gives the scale its own linear predictor -- here
$y_i \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_i, \sigma_i^2)$ with

$$
\mu_i = x_i^{\top} \beta_{\mu} \quad \text{(identity link, the mean model)}

\log \sigma_i = z_i^{\top} \beta_{\sigma} \quad \text{(log link, the scale model)}
$$

so heteroscedasticity is modelled directly (as in `mgcv`'s `gaulss` family).
This is not the scalar IRLS core (one predictor, fixed dispersion), so it gets
its own fitter.

The estimator is joint maximum likelihood by Fisher scoring.  The Gaussian
location-scale expected information is block-diagonal between $\beta_{\mu}$
and $\beta_{\sigma}$ (since $\mathbb{E}[y - \mu] = 0$ kills the
cross term), so each scoring step is two decoupled solves:

$$
\beta_{\mu} \leftarrow (X^{\top} W X)^{-1} X^{\top} W y, \quad W = \operatorname{diag}(1 / \sigma_i^2) \quad \text{(WLS)}

\beta_{\sigma} \leftarrow \beta_{\sigma} + (2 Z^{\top} Z)^{-1} Z^{\top} u, \quad u_i = -1 + (y_i - \mu_i)^2 / \sigma_i^2
$$

iterated to a fixed budget (cleanly vectorised over elements).  The coefficient
covariances are the two information blocks' inverses:
$\operatorname{Cov}(\beta_{\mu}) = (X^{\top} \operatorname{diag}(1/\sigma^2) X)^{-1}$
and $\operatorname{Cov}(\beta_{\sigma}) = (2 Z^{\top} Z)^{-1}$.  Every
solve uses the cuSOLVER-free `small_inv_logdet`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `V` | elements of a mass-univariate map — voxels, vertices, or fixels |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `q` | random-effect columns |
| `N` | observations — time points, subjects, or sampled points |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-stats-gaulss-symbols}
:::
